# [cuDNN][SDPA][Convolution] Expose cuDNN runtime version in CUDA hooks

**Repository:** github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--5811a8d7 (mirror of pytorch)  
**Difficulty:** HARD  
**Category:** cross_module_bug_fix



## Description

cuDNN dispatching heuristics rely on versions checks but currently only that compile-time version is exposed, if we want to allow users to resolve https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/166643 on their end by updating their cuDNN version locally we need to check the runtime version rather than compile-time version.

Test plan: add test comparing performance with cuDNN 9.15+ when disabling and enabling convolutino explicilty for 3D cases.

Also partially covered by smoke test PR: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/165922/files

## Task

Review the PR: [cuDNN][SDPA][Convolution] Expose cuDNN runtime version in CUDA hooks

Description: cuDNN dispatching heuristics rely on versions checks but currently only that compile-time version is exposed, if we want to allow users to resolve https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/166643 on their end by updating their cuDNN version locally we need to check the runtime version rather than compile-time version.

Test plan: add test comparing performance with cuDNN 9.15+ when disabling and enabling convolutino explicilty for 3D cases.

Also partially covered by smoke test PR: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/165922/files

Changes:
- 7 files modified
- 43 additions, 9 deletions

Tasks:
1. Understand the issue being fixed
2. Review the solution in the merged PR
3. Implement the fix to pass all tests
4. Verify your changes compile and match the expected fix

## Success Criteria

Code changes match the expected ground-truth fix.
Code follows repository conventions.
No regressions in existing functionality.
All 7 modified files updated correctly.

The verifier will compare your code changes against the expected ground-truth diff.

## Summary

The PR "Expose cuDNN runtime version in CUDA hooks" adds the ability to check cuDNN's **runtime** version rather than the **compile-time** version (`CUDNN_VERSION` macro).

**7 files changed:**

1. **`aten/src/ATen/detail/CUDAHooksInterface.h`** — Added `versionRuntimeCuDNN()` and `versionCuDNNFrontend()` as virtual methods to the hooks interface

2. **`aten/src/ATen/cuda/detail/CUDAHooks.h`** — Added override declarations for both methods

3. **`aten/src/ATen/cuda/detail/CUDAHooks.cpp`** — Implemented `versionRuntimeCuDNN()` (calls `cudnnGetVersion()` for dynamic linking, falls back to `CUDNN_VERSION` for static linking) and `versionCuDNNFrontend()` (returns `CUDNN_FRONTEND_VERSION`)

4. **`aten/src/ATen/Context.h`** — Exposed both new methods as static methods on `Context`

5. **`aten/src/ATen/native/Convolution.cpp`** — Changed 2 cuDNN version checks from compile-time `versionCuDNN()` to runtime `versionRuntimeCuDNN()` (for the 3D conv broken range check cuDNN 9.8–9.14)

6. **`aten/src/ATen/native/transformers/cuda/sdp_utils.cpp`** — Updated 2 cuDNN version checks for SDPA to use `versionRuntimeCuDNN()`

7. **`test/nn/test_convolution.py`** — Added `test_conv3d_cudnn_broken` test that verifies 3D conv with cuDNN disabled matches cuDNN enabled (validating the workaround for broken cuDNN 9.8–9.14)

19: try:
20:     from torch._C import _cudnn
21: except ImportError:
22:     _cudnn = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
23: 
24: # Write:
25: #
26: #   torch.backends.cudnn.enabled = False
27: #
28: # to globally disable CuDNN/MIOpen
29: 
30: __cudnn_version: Optional[int] = None
31: 
32: if _cudnn is not None:
33: 
34:     def _init():
35:         global __cudnn_version
36:         if __cudnn_version is None:
37:             __cudnn_version = _cudnn.getVersionInt()
38:             runtime_version = _cudnn.getRuntimeVersion()
39:             compile_version = _cudnn.getCompileVersion()
40:             runtime_major, runtime_minor, _ = runtime_version
41:             compile_major, compile_minor, _ = compile_version
42:             # Different major versions are always incompatible
43:             # Starting with cuDNN 7, minor versions are backwards-compatible
44:             # Not sure about MIOpen (ROCm), so always do a strict check
45:             if runtime_major != compile_major:
46:                 cudnn_compatible = False
47:             elif runtime_major < 7 or not _cudnn.is_cuda:
48:                 cudnn_compatible = runtime_minor == compile_minor
49:             else:
50:                 cudnn_compatible = runtime_minor >= compile_minor
51:             if not cudnn_compatible:
52:                 if os.environ.get("PYTORCH_SKIP_CUDNN_COMPATIBILITY_CHECK", "0") == "1":
53:                     return True
54:                 base_error_msg = (
55:                     f"cuDNN version incompatibility: "
56:                     f"PyTorch was compiled  against {compile_version} "
57:                     f"but found runtime version {runtime_version}. "
58:                     f"PyTorch already comes bundled with cuDNN. "
59:                     f"One option to resolving this error is to ensure PyTorch "
60:                     f"can find the bundled cuDNN. "
61:                 )
62: 
63:                 if "LD_LIBRARY_PATH" in os.environ:
64:                     ld_library_path = os.environ.get("LD_LIBRARY_PATH", "")
65:                     if any(
66:                         substring in ld_library_path for substring in ["cuda", "cudnn"]
67:                     ):
68:                         raise RuntimeError(
69:                             f"{base_error_msg}"
70:                             f"Looks like your LD_LIBRARY_PATH contains incompatible version of cudnn. "
71:                             f"Please either remove it from the path or install cudnn {compile_version}"
72:                         )
73:                     else:
74:                         raise RuntimeError(
75:                             f"{base_error_msg}"
76:                             f"one possibility is that there is a "
77:                             f"conflicting cuDNN in LD_LIBRARY_PATH."
78:                         )
79:                 else:
80:                     raise RuntimeError(base_error_msg)
81: 
82:         return True
83: 
84: else:
85:

87:         return False
88:

90: def version():
91:     """Return the version of cuDNN."""
92:     if not _init():
93:         return None
94:     return __cudnn_version

97: CUDNN_TENSOR_DTYPES = {
98:     torch.half,
99:     torch.float,
100:     torch.double,
101: }
102: 
103: 
104: def is_available():
105:     r"""Return a bool indicating if CUDNN is currently available."""
106:     return torch._C._has_cudnn
107: 
108: 
109: def is_acceptable(tensor):
110:     if not torch._C._get_cudnn_enabled():
111:         return False
112:     if tensor.device.type != "cuda" or tensor.dtype not in CUDNN_TENSOR_DTYPES:
113:         return False
114:     if not is_available():
115:         warnings.warn(
116:             "PyTorch was compiled without cuDNN/MIOpen support. To use cuDNN/MIOpen, rebuild "
117:             "PyTorch making sure the library is visible to the build system."
118:         )
119:         return False
120:     if not _init():
121:         warnings.warn(
122:             "cuDNN/MIOpen library not found. Check your {libpath}".format(
123:                 libpath={"darwin": "DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH", "win32": "PATH"}.get(
124:                     sys.platform, "LD_LIBRARY_PATH"
125:                 )
126:             )
127:         )
128:         return False
129:     return True

132: def set_flags(
133:     _enabled=None,
134:     _benchmark=None,
135:     _benchmark_limit=None,
136:     _deterministic=None,
137:     _allow_tf32=None,
138:     _fp32_precision="none",
139: ):
140:     orig_flags = (
141:         torch._C._get_cudnn_enabled(),
142:         torch._C._get_cudnn_benchmark(),
143:         None if not is_available() else torch._C._cuda_get_cudnn_benchmark_limit(),
144:         torch._C._get_cudnn_deterministic(),
145:         torch._C._get_cudnn_allow_tf32(),
146:         torch._C._get_fp32_precision_getter("cuda", "all"),
147:     )
148:     if _enabled is not None:
149:         torch._C._set_cudnn_enabled(_enabled)
150:     if _benchmark is not None:
151:         torch._C._set_cudnn_benchmark(_benchmark)
152:     if _benchmark_limit is not None and is_available():
153:         torch._C._cuda_set_cudnn_benchmark_limit(_benchmark_limit)
154:     if _deterministic is not None:
155:         torch._C._set_cudnn_deterministic(_deterministic)
156:     if _allow_tf32 is not None:
157:         torch._C._set_cudnn_allow_tf32(_allow_tf32)
158:     if _fp32_precision is not None:
159:         torch._C._set_fp32_precision_setter("cuda", "all", _fp32_precision)
160:     return orig_flags

163: @contextmanager
164: def flags(
165:     enabled=False,
166:     benchmark=False,
167:     benchmark_limit=10,
168:     deterministic=False,
169:     allow_tf32=True,
170:     fp32_precision="none",
171: ):
172:     with __allow_nonbracketed_mutation():
173:         orig_flags = set_flags(
174:             enabled,
175:             benchmark,
176:             benchmark_limit,
177:             deterministic,
178:             allow_tf32,
179:             fp32_precision,
180:         )
181:     try:
182:         yield
183:     finally:
184:         # recover the previous values
185:         with __allow_nonbracketed_mutation():
186:             set_flags(*orig_flags)

189: # The magic here is to allow us to intercept code like this:
190: #
191: #   torch.backends.<cudnn|mkldnn>.enabled = True
192: 
193: 
194: class CudnnModule(PropModule):
195:     def __init__(self, m, name):
196:         super().__init__(m, name)
197: 
198:     enabled = ContextProp(torch._C._get_cudnn_enabled, torch._C._set_cudnn_enabled)
199:     deterministic = ContextProp(
200:         torch._C._get_cudnn_deterministic, torch._C._set_cudnn_deterministic
201:     )
202:     benchmark = ContextProp(
203:         torch._C._get_cudnn_benchmark, torch._C._set_cudnn_benchmark
204:     )
205:     benchmark_limit = None
206:     if is_available():
207:         benchmark_limit = ContextProp(
208:             torch._C._cuda_get_cudnn_benchmark_limit,
209:             torch._C._cuda_set_cudnn_benchmark_limit,
210:         )
211:     allow_tf32 = ContextProp(
212:         torch._C._get_cudnn_allow_tf32, torch._C._set_cudnn_allow_tf32
213:     )
214:     conv = _FP32Precision("cuda", "conv")
215:     rnn = _FP32Precision("cuda", "rnn")
216:     fp32_precision = ContextProp(
217:         _get_fp32_precision_getter("cuda", "all"),
218:         _set_fp32_precision_setter("cuda", "all"),
219:     )
220:

222: # This is the sys.modules replacement trick, see
223: # https://stackoverflow.com/questions/2447353/getattr-on-a-module/7668273#7668273
224: sys.modules[__name__] = CudnnModule(sys.modules[__name__], __name__)

**Summary:**

This commit simplifies the existing decomposition hierarchy
of batch norm ops by adding a single, backend agnostic op:
`batch_norm_with_update`. The existing hierarchy looks like:

```
aten.batch_norm ->
aten._batch_norm_impl_index ->
[
  aten.native_batch_norm ->
  aten._native_batch_norm_legit (export only) ->
  _batch_norm_legit_cpu/cuda (kernels, export only) ->
  _batch_norm_cpu/cuda (kernels)
] OR
[ aten.cudnn_batch_norm ] OR
[ aten.miopen_batch_norm ]
```

Aside from complexity, an important problem with the
above decomposition hierarchy is cuda numerics in
export flows. We observed significantly worse convergence
when training a mobilenetv2-like model when using the
`_batch_norm_cuda` kernel instead of the `cudnn_batch_norm`
kernel. This means users who export their models on CPU
first then move the models to cuda later may silently
see worse accuracies even when cudnn is installed,
because they are using the worse kernel. This ... The following workload fails on Ampere:

```
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
bn = nn.BatchNorm2d(128).cuda()
x = torch.randn([256, 128, 294, 294], device='cuda')
x = bn(x)
print(x.shape)
```

This PR adds a magic number in the `use_cudnn` condition to avoid this failure.
